title: Get the form and apply to renew a passport from the US - Canada.ca
content:
Renew a passport from the US

## Get the form and submit your application
Where are you right now? Canada | US | Outside Canada and the US

## Passport and travel document service fees have increased
As of March 31, 2026 , most fees for passports and travel document services have increased. Learn how these changes may affect your application .
Passport applications from the US have to be mailed to Canada.
We aim to process applications from the US within 20 business days , plus mailing time.
- Contact the nearest Government of Canada office abroad for help .
- There are additional fees for urgent requests .

## How to renew your passport
Most people need only the main application form.
- Fill out the form on your computer to save time. Save the form on your computer and use Adobe Reader 10 or higher to open it. Don’t use your phone or tablet, or open it in your web browser. The form will not work properly.
- Save the form on your computer and use Adobe Reader 10 or higher to open it.
- Don’t use your phone or tablet, or open it in your web browser. The form will not work properly.
- Read the instructions at the end of the form to make sure you’ve filled it out properly and completely. Don’t forget to tell us if you want a 5- or 10-year passport under section 4 of the form ( Period of validity ).
- Don’t forget to tell us if you want a 5- or 10-year passport under section 4 of the form ( Period of validity ).
- Include your email address to make it easier to check the status of your application online after you apply.
Download the adult passport renewal application form [PPTC 054] (PDF, 1.64 MB)
Tell us on your form if you need the visa and want your original passport back. If you don’t, the visa could be damaged when the previous passport is cancelled.
- In Canada or the US Contact us online (opens in a new tab) or call 1-800-567-6868 (TTY: 1-866-255-7655). Tell us whether you need a large print form or braille instructions, and your mailing address. We’ll mail you the forms.
- Tell us whether you need a large print form or braille instructions, and your mailing address. We’ll mail you the forms.
- Outside Canada and the US: contact the closest Canadian government office abroad .
You may be eligible to replace your passport photo.
Find out how to change your passport photo .
Lost, stolen or damaged passports may need an extra form
You need to contact us as soon as possible to report the theft or loss of your previous passport.
Since your passport is still valid, you must
- submit a declaration concerning a lost, stolen, inaccessible, damaged or found Canadian travel document [PPTC 203] (PDF, 1.3 MB) with your application
- pay the extra $45 fee for replacing a lost or stolen passport
You only need the main application form . Complete it and then submit it to us.
You must submit a declaration concerning a lost, stolen, inaccessible, damaged or found Canadian travel document [PPTC 203] (PDF, 1.33 MB) with your application.
You only need the main application form . Complete it and then submit it to us.

### Submit your application

## Submit the application by mail
Use a certified courier or traceable mail service to reduce the delivery time of your application and help protect your documents.
Courier address Government of Canada Passport Program 22 de Varennes Street Gatineau, QC  J8T 8R1 Canada
Mailing address (non-courier) Government of Canada Passport Program Gatineau, QC  K1A 0G3 Canada
Your fees depend on what services you’re getting. How you can pay them depends on how you’re applying.
Make sure you pay the correct fees, listed below.
5-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
10-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
There are other fees for replacing a lost or stolen  passport that’s still valid.
Additional fee for replacing a lost or stolen passport that was still valid You must pay this fee if you report a passport as lost or stolen and are applying for a replacement. This fee does not include the cost of the new passport.
Additional fee for replacing a lost or stolen passport that was still valid You must pay this fee if you report a passport as lost or stolen and are applying for a replacement. This fee does not include the cost of the new passport.
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
How to pay your fees by mail
- fill out the payment section on your application form with your credit card (Visa ® , MasterCard ® or American Express ® ) information, or
- include a certified cheque or money order (postal or bank) in Canadian funds with the exact amount
- with the exact amount
All cheques must be made payable to the Receiver General for Canada.
We don’t accept cash or personal cheques.
You won’t get a refund for the service fee if you:
- cancel your application
- are refused a travel document
- are issued a passport for a shorter validity period than requested For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
- For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
- This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
Only the consular fees (opens in a new tab) , which apply to all adult applications, are refundable if a passport is not issued.
